title: Pentagon Misses Deadline on 46 Classified UAP Videos; House Oversight: 'How Convenient'
detail: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-FL), Chair of the House Oversight Task Force on the Declassification of Federal Secrets, had sent a March 31 letter to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth demanding 46 specific classified UAP videos by April 14. The Pentagon missed the deadline entirely and had not responded until Luna's office reached out directly. Luna stated: 'No one from the Pentagon had responded until we reached out, and it appears that someone did not pass the letter to the appropriate authorities. How convenient.' The 46 requested videos — referenced in whistleblower testimony at the September 9, 2025 House Oversight hearing — include footage of spherical objects over Afghanistan, cigar-shaped craft, Tic Tac-style encounters, transmedium vehicles, UAP formations near Iran and Syria, and incidents near U.S. military bases. Investigative journalist Jeremy Corbell had shared specific file locations with Congress. Luna stated: 'We are not waiting for a briefing.'
confidenceReason: Luna's letter to Hegseth is on the public record; Pentagon non-response confirmed by multiple outlets; deadline date confirmed

media.caption: Pentagon fails to deliver 46 classified UAP videos by Luna's April 14 deadline

title: War Department Confirms White House Coordinating 'Never-Before-Seen' UAP Material Release
detail: Following the missed Luna deadline, a War Department official confirmed to Liberation Times that AARO is 'working in close coordination with the White House and across federal agencies to consolidate existing UAP records collections and facilitate the expeditious release of never-before-seen UAP information.' The statement was the Pentagon's first substantive public acknowledgment that previously unseen UAP material exists and is being prepared for release. The coordination reference pointed to Trump's February 19, 2026 executive order process. No timeline for specific releases was provided. Critics noted the statement lacked any firm commitment and followed only after congressional pressure.
confidenceReason: Official War Department spokesperson statement; confirmed by multiple outlets

media.caption: War Department confirms White House coordination on UAP records release
